name: Jon Hendricks
description: American jazz singer, lyricist, jazz critic and historian (1921–2017)
abstract: John Carl Hendricks (September 16, 1921 – November 22, 2017), known professionally as Jon Hendricks, was an American jazz lyricist and singer. He is one of the originators of vocalese, which adds lyrics to existing instrumental songs and replaces many instruments with vocalists, such as the big-band arrangements of Duke Ellington and Cou…

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: So instead of three people singing, it would be that much more. We did that. That’s – one of the greatest moments in my life was standing in front of the speaker and hearing the basic track that we recorded... We – I think we recorded two or three other songs... about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Cascadu
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee never mentions Jon Hendricks; the name is only introduced by the interviewer in the final turn.
about_quotes.doc_title: Cascadu interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, November 15, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Marshall
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Jon in “Evolution of the Blues,” that was in the seventies also.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Marshall interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Giacomo Gates
about_quotes.evidence: I had a wonderful opportunity to sing a few times with Jon Hendricks, Aria Hendricks and Kevin Burke, when it was Lambert, Hendricks, uh, Jon Hendricks and Company.
about_quotes.doc_title: Giacomo Gates interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, March 29, 2015 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Wormworth
about_quotes.evidence: I was their second drummer. I took Walter Bolden’s place... Jon Dave and Annie didn’t overdub anything they just sang along with the band... they hired me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Wormworth interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: Dave Lambert, who introduced me to Jon Hendricks, and that’s how “Sing a Song of Basie” came about... we were a big hit... it was the three of us as one.
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001
